6 - Unaware Prompt

WHEN TO USE UNAWARE MARKET PROMPTS
CRITICAL 2025 CONTEXT:
Unless you're inventing something for the first time, default to Unaware
copy 80% of the time.
Why? Markets are hyper-saturated. Even "aware" prospects have built
walls. They've heard every claim, tried solutions, been burned. Direct
problem ‚Üí solution approaches get filtered out immediately.
The modern paradox:
They KNOW problems exist (weight loss, business growth, relationship
issues). But they're functionally unaware because:
‚óè‚Äã They believe they're already doing what works ("I eat healthy, I work
out")
‚óè‚Äã They've resigned themselves ("This is just how my body is now")
‚óè‚Äã They think real solutions don't exist ("All diets fail eventually")
‚óè‚Äã They're managing symptoms instead of solving root cause
You're targeting Unaware when:
Your prospect doesn't know they have a problem, doesn't know solutions
exist, or doesn't believe their situation is solvable. They're not searching
because they don't know there's a question.
Why direct approaches fail (Schwartz, Breakthrough Advertising pg
34-40):
A direct statement triggers immediate resistance. Say "Your cat is
dehydrated and needs this" and they think: "My cat drinks water, I switched
to wet food, I'm doing fine." You've lost them.
The goal of Unaware copy:

Build awareness gradually through identification and emotion BEFORE
introducing any solution:
‚óè‚Äã Make them recognize themselves in the story (identification)
‚óè‚Äã Give words to feelings they've had but never articulated
‚óè‚Äã Reveal hidden problems through their own realization (not your claim)
‚óè‚Äã Guide them to discover truth themselves (gradualization)
Schwartz's warning (pg 37):
"You cannot sell a man who isn't listening. In an Unaware market, your
prospect is not listening for solutions. They're living their life. Your only job
is to make them stop, recognize themselves, and realize something they
didn't know before."
When to use each awareness level:
‚óè‚Äã Unaware (80%): They're not searching, don't believe they have a
problem, think current approach works, or have resigned themselves
to failure
‚óè‚Äã Problem-Aware (15%): They're in active crisis, know something's
wrong RIGHT NOW, desperately searching (rare in cold traffic)
‚óè‚Äã Solution-Aware (5%): They're comparing you to competitors, just
need to know why yours is better (warm traffic only)
Bottom line:
Your job isn't to sell‚Äîit's to wake them up to a reality they're currently blind
to. The sale happens AFTER the awakening, never before.
In 2025, assume Unaware unless you have clear evidence otherwise.

CRITICAL HEADLINE RULES FOR STAGE 5 (UNAWARE):

‚ùå

‚úÖ

RULE 1: NEVER NAME THE PROBLEM OR SOLUTION
DON'T mention: kidney disease,
UTI, blockage, crystals, dehydration, water fountain, hydration
DO call out: emotions,
behaviors, identity, consequences, observations
RULE 2: USE DIVERSE CLASSIC FORMATS Don't default to "To [WHO] Who [BEHAVIOR]"
for every headline. Rotate through ALL patterns below. Maximum 2 headlines can use the same
pattern.
RULE 3: BALANCE SPECIFICITY
‚óè‚Äã Too broad: "To Anyone Who Worries About Their Pet" (could be any pet owner)
‚óè‚Äã Too specific/problem-aware: "To Cat Owners Who've Had A Blockage" (names the
problem)
‚óè‚Äã PERFECT: "Who Else Counts Wet Spots In The Litter Box?" (specific behavior, no
problem named)
RULE 4: TEST EACH HEADLINE Before including a headline, ask:
1.‚Äã Does this name a medical condition or problem? (If YES ‚Üí reject)
2.‚Äã Would someone WITHOUT this problem still read it? (If NO ‚Üí reject)
3.‚Äã Does the avatar recognize themselves? (If NO ‚Üí reject)
4.‚Äã Have I already used this pattern twice? (If YES ‚Üí use different pattern)

EXAMPLE SET (DO NOT COPY - THESE ARE
EXAMPLES):
Headline 1: A Little Habit That Cost A Cat Owner $5,400‚Äã
Pattern: A LITTLE [THING] THAT COST [PERSON] [BIG CONSEQUENCE]‚Äã
Specificity Check: Financial consequence hooks curiosity without naming what caused it.‚Äã
Why it works: Uses the "Traumatic Number" from Avatar research. "Little habit" implies
something preventable/controllable. Creates curiosity about what habit = what cost.
Headline 2: They Laughed When I Said I Check The Litter Box Every Hour ‚Äì But When They
Saw Why...‚Äã
Pattern: THEY LAUGHED WHEN I [ACTION] ‚Äì BUT WHEN [RESULT]...‚Äã
Specificity Check: Specific behavior (hourly checking) that's "crazy" but has a legitimate
reason.‚Äã
Why it works: Validates the hypervigilant behavior as necessary, not neurotic. Social proof
format creates curiosity about the "why." Avatar 2 does this exact behavior.
Headline 3: Who Else Counts Wet Spots In The Litter Box?‚Äã
Pattern: WHO ELSE [SPECIFIC BEHAVIOR]?‚Äã
Specificity Check: Extremely specific to urinary-monitoring cat owners without naming the
medical fear.‚Äã
Why it works: "Who else" creates community identification. Normalizes what feels like
shameful/obsessive behavior. Avatar 2 does this daily.

Using the inputs, construct the strategic foundation:
The One Belief Formula: "This [NEW OPPORTUNITY] is the key to [THEIR DESIRE - Level 2
Emotional Drive] and it's only attainable through [NEW MECHANISM]"
Extract:
‚óè‚Äã New Opportunity: [From Q5 - what makes it different]
‚óè‚Äã Their Desire: [From Q15 Level 2 - the EMOTIONAL drive, not surface want]
‚óè‚Äã New Mechanism: [From Q4 - how it actually works]

Example: "Intermittent fasting (new opportunity) is the key to feeling confident in your body
again and having your spouse look at you the way they used to (Level 2 emotional desire) and
it's only attainable through the 16:8 protocol that flips your body into fat-burning mode
(mechanism)"
NOT: "Intermittent fasting (new opportunity) is the key to losing 30 pounds (Level 1 surface
want)..."
The One Belief must be built around Level 2 (emotional drive), not Level 1 (surface want).

UPDATED STEP 2B FOR UNAWARE
STORY LEAD
Replace your current Step 2b "Engineer The Gradualization Sequence" with this:

Step 2b: Engineer The Gradualization Sequence
Using the belief shift from Q1 (old belief) ‚Üí Q3 (new belief), YOU must engineer the
gradualization path. DO NOT allow the copy to state the new belief directly. Break the belief shift
into discoverable micro-steps.
REQUIRED: Map out 5-7 micro-steps that lead from old belief to new belief:
Micro-Step 1: Introduce Small Doubt What tiny anomaly challenges the old belief? Frame as
confusion, not revelation Example: "But I noticed Sarah's cat was on wet food and STILL got
diagnosed..."
Micro-Step 2: Show Pattern The anomaly isn't isolated‚Äîit happens to others Example:
"Started asking around. Lot of cats on wet food still get kidney disease."
Micro-Step 3: Create Question What question does this pattern raise? Example: "If wet food
prevents it, why are so many cats on wet food still getting sick?"
Micro-Step 4: Introduce New Information (CRITICAL - Choose the Right Tier)
The TYPE of new information determines how powerful the belief shift is.
THE 4 TIERS OF NEW INFORMATION (Universal Framework):

TIER 1 - Reframe Existing Observable Problem (MOST POWERFUL)

‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Take something they already experience and explain it differently
Makes them realize "I've been looking at this wrong the whole time"
No new problem to create awareness of - just new understanding of existing reality
They can often verify/observe this themselves immediately

Examples for Unaware:
‚óè‚Äã "Why cats on wet food diets still get kidney disease - it's not the food quality, it's the
moisture gap"
‚óè‚Äã "Why sleeping separately becomes permanent - bodies forget each other's warmth,
oxytocin production stops"
‚óè‚Äã "Why you're exhausted by 3pm even when you 'didn't do much' - attention residue from
40+ task switches"
Power: Highest. Reframes their lived experience. Often immediately verifiable. Best for: Any
awareness level, especially Unaware (helps them see what's already happening)

TIER 2 - Explain Why Previous Solutions Failed (VERY POWERFUL)
‚óè‚Äã Reveals why the solutions they tried (or think would work) couldn't work
‚óè‚Äã Removes self-blame and validates their experience
‚óè‚Äã Shows it wasn't their fault - the solutions were fundamentally incomplete
Examples for Unaware:
‚óè‚Äã "Water fountains can't work because they only change delivery, not what cats' biology
recognizes as hydration"
‚óè‚Äã "Date nights don't fix it because the problem isn't time together - it's that your bodies
have forgotten each other"
‚óè‚Äã "To-do lists don't address attention residue - your brain is still processing the last task"
Power: Very high. Explains pattern of failure. Creates relief ("it wasn't me"). Best for: Unaware,
Problem-Aware, Solution-Aware (validates why nothing worked)

TIER 3 - Introduce New Mechanism for Familiar Problem (POWERFUL)
‚óè‚Äã Shows there's a different way to solve what they know (or vaguely sense) needs solving
‚óè‚Äã Reveals a mechanism/process they didn't know existed
‚óè‚Äã Positions your solution as different approach, not better execution
Examples for Unaware:
‚óè‚Äã "Prey-model electrolytes trigger cats' biological hydration recognition at cellular level"

‚óè‚Äã "Tactile reactivation - 15 minutes of intentional touch retrains nervous systems to
recognize each other"
‚óè‚Äã "23-minute transition buffers between tasks eliminate attention residue, restore deep
focus"
Power: Good. Offers new path forward. Best for: Problem-Aware, Solution-Aware (reveals new
solution path)

TIER 4 - Create Awareness of Problem They Didn't Know Existed (LEAST POWERFUL BUT SOMETIMES NECESSARY FOR UNAWARE)
‚óè‚Äã Introduces entirely new problem they weren't worried about
‚óè‚Äã Requires building awareness before they care
‚óè‚Äã Highest resistance because "I wasn't worried about this before"
Examples for Unaware:
‚óè‚Äã "Chronic dehydration causes kidney damage years before symptoms appear - by the
time cats show signs, 75% of kidney function is gone"
‚óè‚Äã "Sleeping separately for 6+ months rewires your nervous systems to sleep alone - 73%
of couples never return to same bed"
‚óè‚Äã "Task-switching creates cognitive residue that accumulates - by 3pm you're processing
40+ incomplete tasks simultaneously"
Power: Lowest for most markets. Requires most education and proof. BUT: Often necessary for
Unaware markets where they don't even know the problem exists. Best for: Unaware markets
specifically (when creating problem awareness is required)

FOR UNAWARE MARKETS:
Best: Tier 1 or Tier 4
‚óè‚Äã Tier 1: Reframe something they already observe (most powerful)
‚óè‚Äã Tier 4: Create awareness of hidden problem (necessary when they're truly unaware)
Good: Tier 2 or Tier 3
‚óè‚Äã Tier 2: If they've tried things (validates failure)
‚óè‚Äã Tier 3: If they vaguely sense something's wrong (offers mechanism)
Ideal sequence for Unaware:
1.‚Äã Tier 1 or 4 in Micro-Step 4 (reframe reality OR create awareness)

2.‚Äã Tier 2 if applicable (why their attempts failed)
3.‚Äã Tier 3 in Micro-Step 5 (your mechanism)

YOUR JOB in Step 2b:
Identify which tier the new information [Q2 from angle, or Q3 from research] falls into
State it: "The new information is Tier [X]: [Type]"
Explain: "This works for Unaware because: [1-2 sentence explanation]"
Example: "The new information is Tier 1: Reframe Existing Observable Problem. This works for
Unaware because cat owners can immediately check their wet food label, see it shows 42%
moisture, realize cats need 70%, and understand the gap exists in their home right now. They
can verify it in 60 seconds without trusting me first."

Now continue mapping Micro-Steps 4-7:
Micro-Step 4 continued: How to present this new information How will you present this
information based on its tier? What can they observe/verify themselves? What authority
validates it? [Q9] What makes it undeniable? Example: "Found UC Davis study: Cats need 70%
moisture for kidney function. Checked my cat's wet food label: 42% moisture."
Micro-Step 5: Connect to Their Situation How does new information apply to their specific
case? Example: "Checked my cat's wet food label: 42% moisture. Even premium brands top out
at 45%."
Micro-Step 6: Show Gap What's the difference between what they thought and what's true?
Example: "Wet food is BETTER than dry, but still leaves a 25-30% moisture gap."
Micro-Step 7: State New Belief as Discovery Frame as realization, not claim Example: "That's
when I realized: wet food alone isn't enough. The gap is still there."
Each micro-step will become 3-5 paragraphs in the actual copy. Total pacing: Spend paragraphs
11-30 walking through these steps.
CRITICAL: The reader should feel like they're discovering this WITH you, not being sold TO.

END OF UPDATED STEP 2B
Step 3: Validate Alignment

Before writing, check:
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Does mechanism address their belief about the problem? [Q7 vs Q4]
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Is the new information Tier 1, 2, or 3? (Tier 4-5 require more market sophistication)
[Q3]
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Can they observe/verify the new information in their own life? (Tier 1 is strongest)
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Does One Belief target Level 2 emotional desire? [Q15 Level 2]
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Does discovery story use their actual language? [Q16]
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Does enemy match existing resentment? [Q17]
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Are failed solutions THEIR actual attempts? [Q6]
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Have you identified the pivot moment? [Q15 - The Pivot Moment]
If any misalignment exists, flag it to the user before proceeding.
Step 4: Map To The 10 Questions
Using the research inputs, prepare to answer these 10 questions sequentially in the copy:
1.‚Äã How is this different? [From Q5]
2.‚Äã What's in it for me? [From Q11, Q15 - BOTH Level 1 and Level 2]
3.‚Äã How do I know this is real? [From Q8, Q9, Q12]
4.‚Äã What's holding me back? [From Q6, Q7]
5.‚Äã Who/What is to blame? [From Q17]
6.‚Äã Why now? [From Q18]
7.‚Äã Why should I trust you? [From Q20]
8.‚Äã How does it work? [From Q4, Q9]
9.‚Äã How can I get started? [From Q19]
10.‚ÄãWhat do I have to lose? [From Q11, Q18]
CRITICAL FOR Q#2: Q#2 must answer BOTH levels of desire:
‚óè‚Äã Surface benefit (what they get) [Q15 Level 1]
‚óè‚Äã Emotional payoff (why they actually want it) [Q15 Level 2]
‚óè‚Äã This is where the emotional pivot happens.

PART 6: WRITING INSTRUCTIONS
Now write the ad copy following these principles:
CORE STRUCTURE: The 10-Question Framework
Your copy must answer all 10 questions in sequence. Each question builds on the previous one.
Flow:

‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Q#1-3: Opening/Lead (first 1/3) - Hook, promise, proof
Q#4-6: Problem crystallization (middle 1/3) - Why they failed, enemy, urgency
Q#7-8: Authority & mechanism (middle 1/3) - Credibility, how it works
Q#9-10: Offer & close (final 1/3) - No-brainer offer, push-pull close

LENGTH & PACING
Target: 2,500-3,000 words (MAXIMUM 3,000)
Do NOT exceed 3,000 words. Tight is better than comprehensive.
Pacing discipline:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Each emotional beat appears maximum 2x
Failed solutions: 2-3 examples max
Discovery story: Key moments only, not play-by-play
Authority dialogue: One good explanation
Transformation timeline: 4-5 data points max

If running long, cut:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Repetitive emotional beats
Extra failed solution examples
Over-detailed discovery story
Multiple authority conversations
Excessive transformation data points

OPENING: Story Lead (Confession Style)
Use the confession-style story lead for Stage 5 (Unaware) markets.
Structure:
1. Identification headline [From Q1, Q2, Q15 Level 1]
‚óè‚Äã Call out their surface problem or hidden emotional state
‚óè‚Äã Make them think: "That's me. How did they know?"
2. Confession opening [From Q2]
‚óè‚Äã "There's a moment [specific time] I've never told anyone about..."
‚óè‚Äã Extreme specificity: exact times, physical sensations, internal thoughts
3. The shameful moment [From Q10]
‚óè‚Äã What were they doing?
‚óè‚Äã What were they feeling?

‚óè‚Äã What were they thinking?
4. Public vs. Private contrast [From Q3]
‚óè‚Äã What they say to others
‚óè‚Äã What they actually feel
‚óè‚Äã The gap between them
THE EMOTIONAL PIVOT (CRITICAL - Must Happen By Paragraph 7)
From Schwartz (Breakthrough Advertising, pg 52): "There are always TWO levels to every
desire: the stated want and the emotional drive. Your headline identifies Level 1. Your opening
body copy must IMMEDIATELY pivot to Level 2, or you'll get attention but no sales."
From Evaldo (16-Word Sales Letter, pg 37-39): "This is where most copywriters fuck up. They
list surface benefits. NOBODY CARES ABOUT THOSE THINGS. They care about what those
things GIVE them emotionally. The surface benefit is the MECHANISM for the emotional payoff.
If you don't connect to the emotional payoff in Q#2, they won't buy. Period."
From Masterson (Great Leads, pg 78-79): "Weak story leads show only the surface
transformation. Strong story leads show the EMOTIONAL transformation that the surface
change created. Show the surface transformation, but SELL the emotional one. That's what
people actually buy."
ALL THREE FRAMEWORKS TEACH: Surface problem hooks attention. Deep emotion drives
purchase.
Required pivot structure:
Paragraphs 1-3: Surface Problem Identification
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Open with confession about surface issue
Build recognition ("that's me")
Show public vs private split
Establish the Level 1 stated want

Paragraphs 4-7: THE PIVOT (CRITICAL - THIS IS Q#2)
‚óè‚Äã "But here's what [nobody tells you / I didn't expect / I didn't understand] about [surface
problem]..."
‚óè‚Äã Reveal Level 2 desire (the emotional drive) [From Q15 Level 2]
‚óè‚Äã Use the pivot moment [From Q15 - The Pivot Moment]
‚óè‚Äã Make it physical/visceral (phantom weight, flinching at touch, tightness in chest)
‚óè‚Äã Show specific moments that illustrate the emotional loss
‚óè‚Äã This is what they're actually buying for
‚óè‚Äã This answers Q#2: "What's in it for me?" (BOTH surface AND emotional)

After the pivot, everything serves the emotional desire:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

All proof should show emotional transformation, not just surface results
Mechanism should explain how it enables emotional payoff
Failed solutions failed because they didn't address emotional need
Offer should emphasize emotional benefit

Pivot language examples:
‚óè‚Äã "But here's what nobody tells you about [surface problem]: You don't just lose [obvious
thing]. You lose [deep emotional thing]."
‚óè‚Äã "I thought it was about [surface problem]. It wasn't. It was about [deeper emotional
truth]."
‚óè‚Äã "[Surface problem] was manageable. What I couldn't handle was [emotional
consequence]."
‚óè‚Äã "For [timeframe], I told myself it was just about [surface problem]. But the real problem
was [emotional truth]."
The pivot must:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Happen by paragraph 7 LATEST (preferably paragraph 4-5)
Use sensory/physical details (not intellectual explanation)
Show what they're actually losing (not just inconvenienced by)
Make them FEEL it (chest tightness, throat lump, visceral recognition)
Reference specific relationship/life moments that are lost

SCHWARTZ PRINCIPLES (Apply Throughout)
Principle 1: Extreme Specificity

‚ùå

Generic = sounds fake:
"I was stressed and it affected my relationship" Schwartz-level =
sounds real: ‚úì "At 11pm on a Tuesday, I sat in my driveway unwrapping a Big Mac‚Äîmy fourth
drive-through stop that week‚Äîwatching my family through the window"
Required details:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Specific times (but natural - see timestamp authenticity below)
Exact numbers ("fourth time," "three months")
Physical sensations ("tightness in chest," "heart racing")
Concrete actions ("staring at phone," "checking if breathing")
Direct internal dialogue

CRITICAL - TIMESTAMP AUTHENTICITY:

‚ùå Too specific (feels calculated):

‚óè‚Äã "11:34pm" (exact minute)
‚óè‚Äã "forty-seven minutes" (hyper-precise)
‚óè‚Äã "3:17am" (oddly specific)
‚úì Specific but natural:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"Late at night" or "After 11pm"
"Almost an hour" or "close to an hour"
"Middle of the night" or "early morning"
"Tuesday evening" (day + general time)

Rule: Use round numbers ("fifteen minutes," "half an hour") or general time windows.
Down-to-the-minute precision feels performative.
Principle 2: ABT (And-But-Therefore) Structure
Use for proof sections and story beats:
‚óè‚Äã AND: Set up background, create momentum
‚óè‚Äã BUT: Introduce conflict/tension (the critical element)
‚óè‚Äã THEREFORE: Present solution
Example: "I tried everything AND spent thousands on solutions BUT nothing worked until I
discovered the real problem THEREFORE I finally lost 34 pounds"
Principle 3: Intensification
Make them feel the pain MORE before offering solution.
Three methods (use all three):
1.‚Äã Verbalization - Make them SAY internally what they feel: [Use exact quotes from Q10,
Q16]‚Äã
‚óã‚Äã "Every morning I check if she's breathing"
‚óã‚Äã "Every time she sleeps too still, I panic"
2.‚Äã Multiplication - Show it's happening MORE than realized: [Use frequency from Q10]‚Äã
‚óã‚Äã "Not just once, but 3-4 times per night"
‚óã‚Äã "Not just checking once, but every single hour"
3.‚Äã Dramatization - Show future pain if unsolved: [Use consequences from Q18]‚Äã
‚óã‚Äã "By the time symptoms show, 75% of kidney function is gone"
‚óã‚Äã "She won't be this young forever"
Principle 4: Gradualization

Lead them step-by-step. Each step is small.
DO NOT jump from: "Random stranger" ‚Üí "Buy my product"
DO lead through:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Step 1: "You're not alone in this fear" (identification)
Step 2: "It's not your fault" (remove guilt)
Step 3: "There's a reason it's happening" (awareness)
Step 4: "There's a solution" (hope)
Step 5: "Here's how it works" (mechanism)
Step 6: "Here's how to get it" (offer)

Principle 5: Failed Solutions Stack
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

List 3-5 things [From Q6]
Include costs
Say why each failed [From Q7]
Build to desperation

Example from Evaldo (16-Word Sales Letter): "I'd tried everything: hired three different coaches
at $2,000 each, bought every course on Clickbank, attended two $5,000 seminars, tried to DIY it
with YouTube videos.
Nothing worked because none of them addressed the actual problem‚ÄîI didn't have a traffic
problem. I had a conversion problem."
Principle 6: Authority Validation (NOT Testimonials)
Authority figure INSIDE the story explains mechanism.
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Authority enters naturally [From Q9]
They explain WHY it works (mechanism)
In plain language
Reframes from "character flaw" to "solvable problem"

Example from Great Leads (Masterson): "My doctor leaned forward.
'What you're experiencing isn't willpower failure. When you restrict calories too severely, your
body interprets it as starvation. Your metabolism slows down to conserve energy. That's why
you're exhausted and why the weight comes back.'
She paused.

'If we fix your metabolism first, the weight comes off naturally. You're not broken‚Äîyour approach
has been backwards.'"
Principle 7: The Accidental Discovery
Never: "I searched for a solution and found this" Always: "I stumbled on this by accident..."
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Unexpected circumstance [From Q8]
Surprising result
Confusion (what's different?)
Investigation (aha moment)
Verification test (proves causation)

Example from 16-Word Sales Letter (Evaldo): "I wasn't even trying to fix my business. I was just
helping my friend debug his sales funnel because he'd bought me dinner.
While I was in his account, I noticed something weird. His conversion rate was 8%. Mine was
2%.
Same traffic source. Same niche. Same offer structure.
'What are you doing differently?' I asked.
He showed me one small change in his checkout sequence.
I tested it in my funnel that night. Conversion rate jumped to 7% within 48 hours."
Principle 8: Transformation Timeline with Metrics
Vague (weak): "Things got better and I felt great"
Schwartz-style (strong): [Use timeline from Q12]
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"First night: [specific result]
Day 3: [observable change]
Week 1: [measurable improvement]
Week 2: [external validation - what others noticed]
Week 3: [behavior that stopped]
Month 1: [emotional shift through action]
Month 3: [current state]"

Example from Breakthrough Advertising (Schwartz): "Day 1: Lost 2 pounds (water weight, but
motivating) Day 3: Stopped feeling hungry between meals Week 1: Down 4 pounds, energy
returning Week 2: Wife commented 'you seem happier' Week 3: Ordered smaller pants Month 1:

Down 15 pounds, stopped avoiding mirrors Month 3: Lost 32 pounds total, feel like myself
again"
Required:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Specific timeframes (not "soon" but "Day 3")
Observable changes (not "felt better" but "lost 4 pounds")
External validation (what others noticed)
Behaviors that stopped
Emotional shifts through actions

FRAMEWORK DISCIPLINE (CRITICAL)
Do NOT add extra emotional beats between framework sections.
FORBIDDEN patterns:
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå Establishing panic ‚Üí Public/private split ‚Üí Panic AGAIN ‚Üí Failed solutions‚Äã

‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Establishing panic ‚Üí Public/private split ‚Üí IMMEDIATELY to failed solutions‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå Failed solutions ‚Üí Extra desperation beat ‚Üí THEN discovery‚Äã

‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Failed solutions ‚Üí IMMEDIATELY to discovery‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå Research at discovery ‚Üí Return home ‚Üí Research AGAIN‚Äã

‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Research ONCE (either at discovery OR after returning, not both)‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå Story ‚Üí Break into criticism/blame ‚Üí Back to story‚Äã

‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Stay IN story, make it personal ("nobody told me"), not systemic ("they know but don't
tell you")‚Äã

Rule: Each beat appears ONCE, maybe twice if critical. Three times = you're circling.
BACKSTORY WARNING (CRITICAL)
Backstory = telling how you got here Present story = what's happening now
Backstory KILLS momentum. Avoid it.
When backstory is acceptable:
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì In the opening (before main crisis starts)

‚óè‚Äã ‚úì As a single sentence callback mid-story
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì When it's literally the discovery moment
When backstory kills your ad:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå Middle of panic spiral
‚ùå Right after emotional peak
‚ùå To justify why something matters
‚ùå More than 50 words

RULE: If subject is universally understood (baby, parent, spouse) ‚Üí NO backstory needed If
subject needs justification (pet, hobby, job) ‚Üí ONE line maximum, or cut it
GRADUALIZATION EXECUTION (Paragraphs 11-30 - CRITICAL)
You mapped the gradualization sequence in Step 2b. Now execute it in the copy.
Paragraphs 11-30: Walk through your 5-7 micro-steps
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Each micro-step = 3-5 paragraphs
Use ABT structure within each step (And-But-Therefore)
Frame as investigation/discovery, not claims
Include physical details, dialogue, specific moments
Use their actual language [Q16]

PACING DISCIPLINE:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

DO NOT state the new belief before paragraph 28
If you're explaining mechanism before paragraph 25, you're going too fast
Each step must feel like natural progression of thought
The doubt must build gradually across 15-20 paragraphs

FORBIDDEN patterns in paragraphs 11-30:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå "I was wrong about [old belief]"
‚ùå "The truth is [new belief]"
‚ùå "Here's what I discovered: [new belief]"
‚ùå Jumping from doubt ‚Üí new belief in under 10 paragraphs
‚ùå Stating the mechanism before building sufficient doubt

REQUIRED patterns:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚úì "But I noticed something weird..." (doubt)
‚úì "That didn't make sense..." (confusion)
‚úì "So I started investigating..." (curiosity)
‚úì "Found a study that stopped me cold..." (new information)

‚óè‚Äã ‚úì "I checked my own [situation]..." (personal application)
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì "That's when it clicked..." (realization)
MICRO-STEP EXECUTION GUIDE:
Micro-Step 1 (Introduce Small Doubt) - Paragraphs 11-13:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Share the specific anomaly that didn't fit
Frame as confusion, not criticism of old belief
Make it concrete: names, situations, specifics
Reader should think: "Huh, that IS weird"

Micro-Step 2 (Show Pattern) - Paragraphs 14-16:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Show this isn't isolated‚Äîhappened to others too
Use actual examples or research [Q8, Q16]
Build from "weird coincidence" to "consistent pattern"
Reader should think: "Why does this keep happening?"

Micro-Step 3 (Create Question) - Paragraphs 17-19:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Articulate the question the pattern raises
Show you investigating, not knowing the answer yet
Include failed attempts to explain it with old belief
Reader should think: "Yeah, what DOES explain this?"

Micro-Step 4 (Introduce New Information) - Paragraphs 20-23:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Present the new information using the tier you identified in Step 2b
If Tier 1 (Observable Proof): Show them what to look at in their own life right now
If Tier 2 (Reframe Problem): Explain their pain differently than they've heard before
If Tier 3 (Why Solutions Failed): Reveal the mechanism that makes past solutions
incomplete
Use authority figure dialogue if available [Q9]
Make it verifiable‚Äîthey should be able to test/observe this
Frame as discovery, not argument
Keep it simple, one key insight per paragraph
Reader should think: "That would explain it..." OR "Holy shit, I can see that right now"

TIER 1 EXECUTION (Observable Proof): Guide them to look at something in their immediate
environment:
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì "Check your cat's water bowl right now. How much is gone?"
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì "Look at your wet food label. What's the moisture percentage?"
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì "Do the math: If your cat eats 6oz of wet food at 42% moisture, that's 2.5oz of
water..." Reader can verify in 60 seconds = belief shift is instant and sticky

TIER 2-3 EXECUTION (Reframe/Failed Solutions): Present research, authority insight, or
mechanism:
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Use authority dialogue [Q9] to explain mechanism
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Connect to their pattern of experience
‚óè‚Äã ‚úì Show why this explains what they've been seeing Reader recognizes pattern = belief
shift through understanding
Micro-Step 5 (Connect to Their Situation) - Paragraphs 24-26:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Apply new information to your specific case
Show the verification moment [Q8]
Include concrete details (checking labels, doing math, testing)
Reader should think: "Wait, that applies to ME too"

Micro-Step 6 (Show Gap) - Paragraphs 27-29:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Reveal the difference between assumption and reality
Quantify the gap if possible
Explain why old belief seemed right but wasn't complete
Reader should think: "Oh my god, I didn't realize..."

Micro-Step 7 (State New Belief as Discovery) - Paragraph 30:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"That's when I realized [new belief]"
Frame as shared discovery
Connect back to their original problem/fear [Q1, Q10]
Reader should think: "Of course! That's why nothing worked!"

SCHWARTZ'S TEST at each micro-step: "Could the reader reject this thought?"
‚óè‚Äã If YES ‚Üí add more setup, slow down, provide more proof
‚óè‚Äã If NO ‚Üí the step is small enough, continue to next step
By paragraph 30, the new belief should feel like the reader's conclusion, not your pitch. The next
sections (mechanism, offer) simply provide the solution to the belief they now hold.
THE ENEMY SECTION (Q#5)
Attack who they already resent [From Q17]
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Describe failed solution in detail
Show why it failed
Reveal who's to blame
Make it "us vs. them"

Example from 16-Word Sales Letter (Evaldo): "And don't get me started on those $2,000
'business coaches.'
I hired three of them.
The first one gave me a 47-page PDF and never answered my emails again. Two grand for a
glorified eBook.
The second one spent six weeks teaching me 'mindset' when what I needed was a funnel that
converted.
The third one kept pushing me to 'invest in more coaching.' His entire business model was
selling more coaching.
I called him out on it.
You know what he said?
'If you're not willing to invest in yourself, you'll never succeed.'
Translation: 'Keep paying me even though nothing's working.'
These people don't care if your business grows.
They care about monthly recurring revenue from desperate entrepreneurs."
URGENCY SECTION (Q#6)
Create urgency through CONSEQUENCE, not fake scarcity [Use consequences from Q18]
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"I'm not trying to scare you, but..."
State the progressive nature of problem
Show window of opportunity
Emphasize time lost can't be recovered

Example from Breakthrough Advertising (Schwartz): "Look, I'm not trying to scare you.
But here's what my accountant told me when I finally hired one:
'Every month you don't fix your bookkeeping, you're paying 30-40% more in taxes than you
should. That money is just... gone. You can't get it back.'
I'd been in business for three years.
I did the math. I'd overpaid roughly $47,000 in taxes because I didn't know what I was doing.

Forty-seven thousand dollars. Gone.
Every month you wait is more money you'll never get back."
MECHANISM SECTION (Q#8)
Explain how it actually works [From Q4]
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"So here's what makes product different..."
Explain mechanism in plain language
Contrast with how competitors work
Tie back to their biology/psychology
Make it believable through authority validation [From Q9]
Use existing beliefs from their world, not jargon.

Example from Great Leads (Masterson): "So here's what makes this approach different from
every diet you've tried.
Most diets focus on WHAT you eat. Count calories. Cut carbs. Eliminate sugar.
That's backwards.
Your body doesn't care about calories. It cares about WHEN you eat.
When you eat throughout the day, your insulin stays elevated. Elevated insulin tells your body:
'Store fat. Don't burn it.'
But when you compress your eating into a shorter window‚Äîsay, 8 hours instead of 16‚Äîyour
insulin drops for extended periods.
Low insulin tells your body: 'Burn fat. We need energy.'
That's why people lose weight even eating the same foods. It's not about what. It's about when.
My doctor explained it like this: 'Your body is either in fat-storage mode or fat-burning mode. You
can't be in both. When you eat constantly, you're in storage mode 16 hours a day. When you
fast, you flip the switch.'"
OFFER SECTION (Q#9)
Structure the no-brainer offer [From Q19]
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã Acknowledge price concern

‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Break down costs (manufacturing reality OR value comparison)
State regular price
Reveal promotional price
List what's included (value stack)
Add bonuses with values
Calculate total value
Show their price
Guarantee (risk reversal)

Example: "Look, I know what you're thinking.
'This sounds expensive.'
And honestly? It should be.
When I was building this course, I hired three different experts. Paid a developer $8,000 to build
the software. Spent six months testing it with beta users.
My all-in cost was over $30,000.
Most course creators would slap a 10x markup on that and call it a day. That'd be $300,000.
But I'm not trying to build a guru empire. I'm trying to help people who were where I was.
So here's what I'm doing:

Because I know once you see results, you'll tell other people. Word of mouth is how I've grown.

But wait‚ÄîI'm also including:
Bonus #1: [Name] ($97 value) [Description]
Bonus #2: [Name] ($67 value) [Description]

Bonus #1: $97 Bonus #2: $67 Bonus #3: $47

Your Price Today: $197

CLOSING: Push-Pull Technique (Q#10)
CRITICAL: Your closing must be ORIGINAL.
FORBIDDEN patterns:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚ùå Using identical phrases from reference ads

‚ùå Future predictions: "She'll get up in a few minutes. Walk over. Drink."
‚ùå Redemption clich√©: "Because I didn't fail [them] after all"
‚ùå Template feeling - if you could swap 5 words for another product, you failed

ACCEPTABLE elements:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

‚úì Direct address (vary the phrasing - not "If you're reading this..." every time)
‚úì Present moment description (without formulaic predictions)
‚úì Reflection on past self
‚úì Insight about what was really happening
‚úì Soft product mention with trial/guarantee
‚úì Brief timeframe contrast (just don't make it the entire structure)

Structure:

‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Give them control ("I can't force you...")
Present options (do nothing, try competitors, get yours)
Be honest about what each option means
No fake scarcity, but real consequence
Soft invitation, not hard push

Example from Evaldo (16-Word Sales Letter): "Here's the thing...
I can't force you to do anything.
You have three options right now:
Option 1: Keep doing what you're doing.
Keep trying to figure it out on your own. Maybe you'll crack the code eventually.
But how much time and money are you willing to lose while you're learning?
Option 2: Hire an expensive coach.
Go find someone who charges $3,000-5,000 for six weeks of their time.
Maybe they'll be good. Maybe they'll just give you another PDF and disappear.
Option 3: Get Product.
For $197, you get the exact system I used to go from broke to $80K/month in eight months.
The same system 2,400+ people have used to get results.
With a 60-day guarantee that means you risk absolutely nothing.
I'll be honest with you...
Some people buy this and never open it. Some people watch the first module and quit.
But the people who actually implement it‚Äîwho follow the steps, do the work, put in the
hours‚Äîthey get results.
I can't guarantee you'll make $80K/month. That'd be illegal.
But I can guarantee that if you follow this system, you'll have a funnel that converts better than
what you have now.
The choice is yours.
I'm not going to hit you with fake scarcity or countdown timers.

This offer is real. The $197 price is for first-time customers. The 60-day guarantee is real.
But I will tell you this:
Every day you wait is another day of losing money on a funnel that doesn't convert.
And I learned the hard way that you can't get that time back.
[CTA button/link]
You'll have a better funnel.
You'll stop bleeding money.
And you'll wish you'd done this sooner.
I know I do.
To your success,
[Signature] [Name] [Credentials from Q20]
P.S. - Remember, you're covered by the 60-day guarantee. If product doesn't help you [result],
send it back for a full refund. You risk nothing."

PROMPT: Proclamation Lead Ad Copy w/

‚óè‚Äã Macro Avatar Document: [ATTACH MACRO AVATAR DOC]

PHASE 1: CLAUDE ANALYSIS & PROCLAMATION IDENTIFICATION
Claude, before generating headlines, you must analyze the uploaded research and identify
potential proclamation angles:
STEP 1: EXTRACT FROM UPLOADED DOCUMENTS
From Macro Avatar Doc, extract:
- Avatar's core desire (Level 1 surface + Level 2 emotional drive)
- Current limiting belief (what keeps them inactive/unaware)
- Dominant emotion/fear
- Failed solutions they've tried (with costs if mentioned)
- Trigger experiences
- Actual customer language (exact quotes/phrases)
From Market Research Doc, extract:
- Additional customer language and pain patterns
- Observable behaviors and symptoms
- Authority validations (vet quotes, studies, expert opinions)
- Enemy/blame targets (who/what they resent)
- Results timelines (when people noticed changes)
From Product Info, extract:
- Core mechanism (how it actually works - technical, not marketing speak)
- Key differentiation (what makes it unique vs. competitors)
- Observable proof/results
From Offer Details (user provided), extract:
- Price and promotional pricing
- What's included in the offer
- Guarantee terms
- Bonuses (if any)
STEP 2: IDENTIFY 3-5 POTENTIAL PROCLAMATION ANGLES
Based on avatar's current state and research, identify 3-5 potential proclamations:

For each proclamation, determine:
**Proclamation Type:**
- TYPE 1: BOLD STATEMENT OF FACT - Shocking statistic/fact that contradicts assumptions
- TYPE 2: BOLD PREDICTION - Future event with timeframe that will impact them
- TYPE 3: INCREDIBLE DISCOVERY - Solution so effective it seems impossible
**Supporting Tier:**
- Tier 1: Reframe observable problem (they can verify RIGHT NOW)
- Tier 2: Explain why previous solutions failed
- Tier 3: Introduce new mechanism for familiar problem
- Tier 4: Create awareness of hidden problem
**Observable Proof/Authority:** What they can verify OR expert validation available
STEP 3: VALIDATE EACH PROCLAMATION
Test each against Masterson's 4 rules:
‚úì Bold Test: Is this startling/contrary enough to grab attention?
‚úì Promise Test: Does this make an implicit or explicit promise?
‚úì Relevance Test: Is this something they care about?
‚úì Proof Test: Can this be proven with research/authority from uploaded docs?
STEP 4: PRESENT OPTIONS TO USER
Output 3-5 proclamation options in this format:
PROCLAMATION OPTIONS IDENTIFIED
**Option 1:**
Avatar: [Name/Number]
Current Belief: "[X]"
Proclamation (Type [X]): "[Your bold statement]"
Supporting Tier: [Tier X - Type]
Observable Proof: "[How they can verify OR authority backing]"

**Option 2:**
[Repeat format]
**Option 3:**
[Repeat format]
[Continue for 3-5 options]
Ask user: "Which proclamation angle do you want to use? (Reply with the option number)"
Once user selects, proceed to Part 4 for headline generation.
END OF PHASE 1

PART 4: HEADLINE GENERATION
Claude, now generate 10-15 headline options based on the research provided above. Use the

PROCLAMATION HEADLINE PATTERNS:
Use Schwartz's patterns combined with Masterson's proclamation types:
1.‚Äã Bold Fact + Emotional Impact: "[Shocking Statistic] That [Emotional Consequence]"
2.‚Äã Bold Prediction with Date: "By [Date/Timeframe], [Dramatic Event] Will [Impact]"
3.‚Äã Incredible Discovery: "The [Thing] That [Impossible-Sounding Result]"
4.‚Äã Contrarian Statement: "Why [Common Belief] Is [Negative Consequence]"
5.‚Äã Shocking Revelation: "[Authority] Says [Unexpected Truth]"
6.‚Äã Future Forecast: "[Number] Things That Will [Dramatic Outcome]"
7.‚Äã Hidden Truth: "What [Authority/They] Won't Tell You About [Topic]"
8.‚Äã Either-Or Fallacy: "Either [Option A] or [Option B]" (creates false dilemma that raises
stakes)
SCHWARTZ'S RULES FOR UNAWARE HEADLINES:

‚úÖ

‚úÖ

‚úÖ

For Unaware Markets:
Call out WHO they are (identification) OR what they care about
Echo a hidden emotion, fear, or unexpected truth
Give words to something they feel but can't
articulate
Be BOLD and startling (not reasonable)
Make it relevant to what they care
about
Do NOT mention the product
Do NOT make it sound like obvious marketing

‚ùå

‚úÖ

‚ùå

‚úÖ

MASTERSON'S RULES FOR PROCLAMATION HEADLINES:
‚úì Bold, Not Reasonable: Must grab attention through shock value ‚úì Implicit Promise: Hints
at solution or relief (doesn't explicitly state it) ‚úì Relevant: Addresses what they deeply care
about ‚úì Provable: Must be backed by research/authority (you'll prove it in body copy)
OUTPUT FORMAT:
Headline 1: [The actual headline] Pattern: [Pattern name/number] Type: [Fact, Prediction, or
Discovery] Why it works: [1-2 sentences explaining psychological impact]
Headline 2: [The actual headline] Pattern: [Pattern name/number] Type: [Fact, Prediction, or
Discovery] Why it works: [1-2 sentences]
[Continue for 10-15 headlines total]

After generating headlines, ask user: "Which headline do you want to use for your ad

User will select a headline number.

PART 5: CLAUDE PROCESSING INSTRUCTIONS
Claude, before writing the ad copy, you must:

Step 1: Extract & Analyze Inputs
If documents were uploaded (Option A):
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Extract primary emotion, desires (both levels), language, beliefs from avatar doc
Identify which of the 5 avatars/mass desires is being targeted
Pull actual customer language from language bank
Extract product mechanism and differentiation

If manual input was provided (Option B):
‚óè‚Äã Use the answers from Q1-Q20 directly

Step 2: Build The One Belief
Using the inputs, construct the strategic foundation:
The One Belief Formula: "This [NEW OPPORTUNITY] is the key to [THEIR DESIRE - Level 2
Emotional Drive] and it's only attainable through [NEW MECHANISM]"
Extract:
‚óè‚Äã New Opportunity: [From Q5 - what makes it different]
‚óè‚Äã Their Desire: [From Q15 Level 2 - the EMOTIONAL drive, not surface want]
‚óè‚Äã New Mechanism: [From Q4 - how it actually works]
Example: "Prey-model electrolyte supplementation (new opportunity) is the key to having years
left with your cat and never wondering if you missed the prevention window (Level 2 emotional
desire) and it's only attainable through the electrolyte formula that triggers cats' biological
hydration recognition at the cellular level (mechanism)"
NOT: "Electrolyte supplementation (new opportunity) is the key to getting your cat to drink more
water (Level 1 surface want)..."
The One Belief must be built around Level 2 (emotional drive), not Level 1 (surface want).

Step 2b: Engineer The Proclamation Proof Sequence
CRITICAL DIFFERENCE from Story Lead: Proclamation Leads spend 20-40% of copy
PROVING the proclamation before revealing solution.
The Proclamation Proof Sequence (Different from Story Gradualization):
Paragraphs 1-5: THE PROCLAMATION
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Headline states the bold claim
Opening 3-5 paragraphs EXPAND on the proclamation
Stir emotions (fear, hope, curiosity)
Make the proclamation FEEL urgent and real
DO NOT prove it yet - just make them NEED to know if it's true

Paragraphs 6-20: PROVE THE PROCLAMATION (CRITICAL - This is unique to
Proclamation Leads) This is where Proclamation differs from Story. You must EXTENSIVELY
prove the bold claim before connecting to their life.
Map out 4-6 proof steps:

Proof Step 1: Introduce Authority/Research
‚óè‚Äã Who discovered this? (researcher, doctor, study)
‚óè‚Äã What were they investigating?
‚óè‚Äã What credentials make them credible?
Proof Step 2: Present The Data
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

What statistics/facts support the proclamation?
What studies were done?
What did they find?
Use specific numbers, percentages, timeframes

Proof Step 3: Show The Pattern
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

This isn't isolated - it's happening widely
Multiple sources confirm it
Examples from different contexts
The evidence is undeniable

Proof Step 4: Explain The Mechanism
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

WHY is the proclamation true?
What biological/psychological/physical process explains it?
Use authority dialogue to explain [Q9]
Make it simple but credible

Proof Step 5: Address Objections
‚óè‚Äã "You might be thinking..."
‚óè‚Äã Preempt doubt
‚óè‚Äã Show why common objections don't hold
Proof Step 6: Restate Proclamation with New Weight
‚óè‚Äã "So when I say [proclamation], here's what I mean..."
‚óè‚Äã Now they believe it because you've proven it
‚óè‚Äã Transition to "here's what this means for YOU"
From Masterson: "Great Proclamation Leads almost always come from research. The trick is
to stay alert for 'amazing' facts as you are doing your reading. When you find something that
strikes you as remarkable, write it down, and go back to it later."
YOUR JOB: Identify the research/authority from Q9 that proves your proclamation. Map out
how you'll present proof in 10-15 paragraphs before connecting to their situation.

Paragraphs 21-35: CONNECT TO THEIR SITUATION Now that they believe the proclamation,
show how it applies to THEM specifically.
Map out 5-7 connection steps:
Connection Step 1: Personal Application
‚óè‚Äã "Here's what this means for you..."
‚óè‚Äã Connect proclamation to their specific situation [Q1, Q10]
‚óè‚Äã Make it immediate and personal
Connection Step 2: Observable Evidence
‚óè‚Äã What can they see/verify right now in their life?
‚óè‚Äã Use Tier 1 if possible (observable proof)
‚óè‚Äã Guide them to look at something specific
Connection Step 3: Hidden Cost
‚óè‚Äã What's been happening without them realizing?
‚óè‚Äã How long has this been going on? [Q18]
‚óè‚Äã What damage has accumulated?
Connection Step 4: Failed Solutions Explained
‚óè‚Äã Why didn't previous attempts work? [Q6, Q7]
‚óè‚Äã Use Tier 2 (explain failure)
‚óè‚Äã Remove self-blame - it wasn't their fault
Connection Step 5: The Choice Point
‚óè‚Äã "You have two options..."
‚óè‚Äã Do nothing (consequence) vs. Act now (possibility)
‚óè‚Äã Make inaction feel costly [Q18]
Connection Step 6: Mechanism Revelation
‚óè‚Äã Your solution addresses the root cause [Q4]
‚óè‚Äã Use Tier 3 (new mechanism)
‚óè‚Äã Contrast with incomplete solutions
Connection Step 7: Transformation Possibility
‚óè‚Äã What becomes possible? [Q11]
‚óè‚Äã Emotional payoff (Level 2 desire)
‚óè‚Äã Specific moments that return

Paragraphs 36-50: SOLUTION + TRANSFORMATION
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Introduce your product naturally
Show how mechanism works [Q4]
Present transformation timeline [Q12]
Authority validation [Q9]
Emotional before/after [Q10, Q11]

Paragraphs 51-65: OFFER + CLOSE
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Present offer [Q19]
Return to proclamation (Rule #4)
Tie guarantee to proclamation
Push-pull close

Step 3: Validate Alignment
Before writing, check:
‚úì Does proclamation pass Masterson's 4 rules? (Bold, Promise, Relevant, Provable) ‚úì Can
proclamation be proven with research/authority? [Q9] ‚úì Is the new information Tier 1, 2, 3, or
4? [from Step 1] ‚úì Does One Belief target Level 2 emotional desire? [Q15 Level 2] ‚úì Have you
mapped 10-15 paragraphs of PROOF before solution? ‚úì Does solution mechanism connect to
proclamation? ‚úì Are failed solutions THEIR actual attempts? [Q6] ‚úì Does enemy match
existing resentment? [Q17] ‚úì Have you identified the pivot moment? [Q15 - The Pivot Moment]
If any misalignment exists, flag it to the user before proceeding.

Step 4: Map To The 10 Questions
Using the research inputs, prepare to answer these 10 questions sequentially in the copy:
Q#1: How is this different? [From Q5] Q#2: What's in it for me? [From Q11, Q15 - BOTH Level 1
and Level 2] Q#3: How do I know this is real? [From Q8, Q9, Q12 - THIS IS WHERE YOU
PROVE THE PROCLAMATION] Q#4: What's holding me back? [From Q6, Q7] Q#5: Who/What
is to blame? [From Q17] Q#6: Why now? [From Q18] Q#7: Why should I trust you? [From Q20]
Q#8: How does it work? [From Q4, Q9] Q#9: How can I get started? [From Q19] Q#10: What do
I have to lose? [From Q11, Q18]
CRITICAL FOR Q#2: Q#2 must answer BOTH levels of desire:
‚óè‚Äã Surface benefit (what they get) [Q15 Level 1]

‚óè‚Äã Emotional payoff (why they actually want it) [Q15 Level 2]
CRITICAL FOR Q#3: This is where Proclamation differs from other leads. Q#3 requires
EXTENSIVE proof (10-15 paragraphs) before moving to Q#4.

PART 6: WRITING INSTRUCTIONS
Now write the ad copy following these principles:

CORE STRUCTURE: The 10-Question Framework (Proclamation Version)
Your copy must answer all 10 questions in sequence. Each question builds on the previous one.
Flow:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Q#1: Opening/Lead (paragraphs 1-5) - Bold proclamation
Q#3: Proof section (paragraphs 6-20) - EXTENSIVE PROOF (unique to Proclamation)
Q#2: Connection (paragraphs 21-25) - What's in it for them
Q#4-6: Problem crystallization (paragraphs 26-40) - Why they failed, enemy, urgency
Q#7-8: Authority & mechanism (paragraphs 41-50) - Credibility, how it works
Q#9-10: Offer & close (paragraphs 51-65) - No-brainer offer, return to proclamation

Note: Proclamation Leads jump straight to proof (Q#3) after opening, then circle back to "what's
in it for me" (Q#2) once proclamation is proven.

LENGTH & PACING
Target: 2,500-3,000 words (MAXIMUM 3,000)
Do NOT exceed 3,000 words. Tight is better than comprehensive.
Pacing discipline:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Proclamation: 5 paragraphs
Proof: 10-15 paragraphs (LONGER than Story Lead - this is critical for Proclamation)
Connection/Mechanism: 15 paragraphs
Solution: 10 paragraphs
Offer: 15 paragraphs

If running long, cut:
‚óè‚Äã Repetitive proof examples

‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Extra failed solution examples
Over-detailed discovery story
Multiple authority conversations
Excessive transformation data points

OPENING: Proclamation Lead (Research-Backed Statement)
Structure:
1. Bold proclamation headline [From Step 1 Q2]
‚óè‚Äã Must be BOLD and startling (not reasonable)
‚óè‚Äã Must be relevant to what they care about
‚óè‚Äã Must be provable (you'll prove it next)
2. Proclamation expansion (Paragraphs 1-5)
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Open with the bold statement
Expand on what it means
Stir emotions (fear, hope, curiosity)
Make it feel urgent and real
DO NOT prove it yet
Make them NEED to know if it's true

Example Opening (Type 1 - Bold Fact):
"75% of Cats Die From Kidney Disease That's 100% Preventable.
By the time symptoms show - the lethargy, the vomiting, the weight loss - 75% of kidney function
is already gone.
And here's what nobody tells you: Early-stage kidney disease is reversible. For 6-12 months,
you can stop it. Even reverse it.
But after that window closes, the damage becomes permanent.
Most cat owners don't know this window exists. They don't know their cat's kidneys are failing
until it's too late to do anything about it.
And the tragedy is - it's 100% preventable."
Example Opening (Type 2 - Prediction):
"By Age 10, Your Cat's Kidneys Will Be 50% Damaged - Unless You Do This One Thing.

It's happening right now. While your cat sleeps on the couch, while they eat their meals, while
they play with their toys.
Their kidneys are slowly, silently failing.
By the time they're 10 years old, the average cat has lost half their kidney function.
By 12, it's 75%.
And by the time you see symptoms - the excessive drinking, the vomiting, the weight loss - it's
too late to reverse.
The damage is permanent.
But there's a 6-12 month window where it's still reversible. Where you can stop it. Even undo it.
The question is: How do you know if you're still in that window?"

THE PROOF SECTION (Paragraphs 6-20 - CRITICAL FOR PROCLAMATION
LEADS)
This is what makes Proclamation different from Story.
From Masterson: "Proclamation Leads depend on efficiency for their success. Usually all the
power is contained in a single declarative sentence. But that sentence must be FOUND through
research, not invented. And once stated, it must be PROVEN extensively before the reader will
trust you."
Spend 20-40% of your copy PROVING the proclamation before revealing the solution.
Structure:
Proof Step 1: Introduce Authority/Research (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã Who discovered this?
‚óè‚Äã What were they investigating?
‚óè‚Äã What credentials make them credible?
Example: "This statistic comes from a 15-year study by UC Davis School of Veterinary
Medicine.
They tracked 3,200 domestic cats from age 3 to age 15.
Every two years, they measured kidney function through bloodwork.

What they found stopped them cold."
Proof Step 2: Present The Data (3-4 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã What statistics/facts support the proclamation?
‚óè‚Äã Use specific numbers, percentages, timeframes
‚óè‚Äã Make it undeniable
Example: "By age 7, 31% of cats showed early-stage chronic kidney disease.
By age 10, it was 52%.
By age 12, 68%.
By age 15, 81%.
But here's what shocked them: In every single case, the kidney damage started years before
any symptoms appeared.
The average cat lost 40-50% of kidney function before owners noticed anything wrong."
Proof Step 3: Show The Pattern (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã This isn't isolated - it's happening widely
‚óè‚Äã Multiple sources confirm it
‚óè‚Äã The evidence is undeniable
Example: "The Banfield Pet Hospital analyzed data from 2.3 million cats.
Same pattern.
A study in the Journal of Feline Medicine and Surgery: Same pattern.
Cornell University's College of Veterinary Medicine: Same pattern.
This isn't controversial. Every major veterinary institution knows this."
Proof Step 4: Explain The Mechanism (3-4 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã WHY is the proclamation true?
‚óè‚Äã What biological process explains it?
‚óè‚Äã Use authority dialogue [Q9]
Example: "I spoke with Dr. Sarah Chen, a veterinary nephrologist at UC Davis.
She explained it like this:

'Cats evolved in deserts. Their kidneys are designed to concentrate urine extremely well - they
can survive on minimal water. But that same adaptation makes them vulnerable. When they're
chronically under-hydrated - which most domestic cats are - their kidneys work overtime. Year
after year of that stress causes cumulative damage.'
She paused.
'By the time we see elevated creatinine levels on bloodwork, 75% of the nephrons are already
destroyed. And once a nephron dies, it doesn't regenerate. The damage is permanent.'
'But here's what most owners don't know: There's a 6-12 month window in early stage where it's
still reversible. If you catch it then and fix the underlying dehydration, kidney function can
improve. Sometimes significantly.'
'The problem is, almost no one catches it in that window. Because there are no symptoms. Your
cat seems fine. They're eating, playing, acting normal. But underneath, the damage is
accumulating.'"
Proof Step 5: Address Objections (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã Preempt doubt
‚óè‚Äã Show why common objections don't hold
Example: "You might be thinking: 'But my cat drinks water. I see her at the bowl.'
Dr. Chen hears this all the time.
'The problem isn't that cats don't drink. It's that they don't drink enough. And even when they do
drink, plain water doesn't hydrate them the way prey does. Their kidneys evolved to process
blood and tissue fluid from mice and birds - fluids rich in electrolytes. Plain water? Their kidneys
don't recognize it as adequate hydration.'"
Proof Step 6: Restate Proclamation with New Weight (1-2 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã Now they believe it
‚óè‚Äã Transition to "here's what this means for YOU"
Example: "So when I say 75% of cats die from kidney disease that's 100% preventable, here's
what I mean:
The disease progresses silently for years. By the time symptoms show, it's too late. But there's a
window - 6 to 12 months - where it's still reversible.
The question is: Is your cat in that window right now?"

CONNECT TO THEIR SITUATION (Paragraphs 21-35)
Now that they believe the proclamation, show how it applies to THEM specifically.
Connection Step 1: Personal Application (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã "Here's what this means for you..."
‚óè‚Äã Connect to their specific situation [Q1, Q10]
‚óè‚Äã Make it immediate
Example: "Here's what this means for you:
If your cat is over 7, there's a 30% chance they already have early-stage chronic kidney
disease.
You can't see it. They're acting normal. But the damage is accumulating.
Every day they're under-hydrated, more nephrons die.
And you won't know until 75% are gone."
Connection Step 2: Observable Evidence (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã What can they see right now?
‚óè‚Äã Use Tier 1 (observable proof) if possible
Example (Tier 1): "Here's a test: How many times did your cat drink water yesterday?
If you can't remember, that's the first sign.
Most cats should drink 6-8 times per day. But the average cat drinks 1-2 times.
Check your cat's water bowl right now. If it's still mostly full from yesterday, your cat is
chronically under-hydrated."
Connection Step 3: Hidden Cost (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã What's been happening without them realizing? [Q18]
Example: "Every day of under-hydration = more kidney damage.
If your cat has been drinking 1-2 times per day for the last three years, that's over 1,000 days of
cumulative stress on their kidneys.
The damage is already done. The question is: How much?"
Connection Step 4: Failed Solutions Explained (3-4 paragraphs)

‚óè‚Äã Why didn't previous attempts work? [Q6, Q7]
‚óè‚Äã Use Tier 2 (explain failure)
Example: "'But I switched to wet food,' you're thinking.
Wet food is better than dry. Absolutely.
But even premium wet food is only 42-45% moisture.
Cats need 70% for optimal kidney function.
That 25-30% gap? Still there.
'But I bought a water fountain.'
Water fountains encourage drinking by making water more appealing (movement).
But they don't change what's IN the water.
Plain water - even moving water - doesn't trigger cats' biological hydration response the way
prey fluids do."
Connection Step 5: The Choice Point (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã Two options: do nothing vs. act
Example: "So you have two options:
Option 1: Do nothing. Hope your cat is one of the lucky ones.
But if they're not - if they're in that 75% - by the time you know for sure, the prevention window
has closed.
Option 2: Fix the hydration gap now. While there's still time."
Connection Step 6: Mechanism Revelation (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã Your solution addresses root cause [Q4]
‚óè‚Äã Use Tier 3 (new mechanism)
Example: "The solution isn't more water.
It's the RIGHT KIND of water.
Cats' kidneys evolved to process electrolyte-rich fluids - blood and tissue fluid from prey.
That's what triggers their biological hydration recognition.

That's what plain water is missing."
Connection Step 7: Transformation Possibility (2-3 paragraphs)
‚óè‚Äã What becomes possible? [Q11]
‚óè‚Äã Emotional payoff (Level 2)
Example: "Imagine waking up tomorrow and NOT checking if your cat is breathing.
Not Googling 'early signs of kidney disease' at 2am.
Not feeling guilty every time you see their water bowl still full.
Just... having years left. Together.
That's what fixing the hydration gap gives you."

SCHWARTZ PRINCIPLES (Apply Throughout)
Principle 1: Extreme Specificity
Generic = sounds fake:

‚ùå "I was stressed and it affected my relationship"

Schwartz-level = sounds real: ‚úì "At 11pm on a Tuesday, I sat in my driveway unwrapping a Big
Mac‚Äîmy fourth drive-through stop that week‚Äîwatching my family through the window"
Required details:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Specific times (but natural - see timestamp authenticity below)
Exact numbers ("fourth time," "three months")
Physical sensations ("tightness in chest," "heart racing")
Concrete actions ("staring at phone," "checking if breathing")
Direct internal dialogue

CRITICAL - TIMESTAMP AUTHENTICITY:

‚ùå Too specific (feels calculated):

‚óè‚Äã "11:34pm" (exact minute)
‚óè‚Äã "forty-seven minutes" (hyper-precise)
‚óè‚Äã "3:17am" (oddly specific)
‚úì Specific but natural:
‚óè‚Äã "Late at night" or "After 11pm"

‚óè‚Äã "Almost an hour" or "close to an hour"
‚óè‚Äã "Middle of the night" or "early morning"
‚óè‚Äã "Tuesday evening" (day + general time)
Rule: Use round numbers ("fifteen minutes," "half an hour") or general time windows.
Down-to-the-minute precision feels performative.

Principle 2: ABT (And-But-Therefore) Structure
Use for proof sections and story beats:
‚óè‚Äã AND: Set up background, create momentum
‚óè‚Äã BUT: Introduce conflict/tension (the critical element)
‚óè‚Äã THEREFORE: Present solution
Example: "I tried everything AND spent thousands on solutions BUT nothing worked until I
discovered the real problem THEREFORE I finally lost 34 pounds"

Principle 3: Intensification
Make them feel the pain MORE before offering solution.
Three methods (use all three):
1. Verbalization - Make them SAY internally what they feel: [Use exact quotes from Q10, Q16]
‚óè‚Äã "Every morning I check if she's breathing"
‚óè‚Äã "Every time she sleeps too still, I panic"
2. Multiplication - Show it's happening MORE than realized: [Use frequency from Q10]
‚óè‚Äã "Not just once, but 3-4 times per night"
‚óè‚Äã "Not just checking once, but every single hour"
3. Dramatization - Show future pain if unsolved: [Use consequences from Q18]
‚óè‚Äã "By the time symptoms show, 75% of kidney function is gone"
‚óè‚Äã "She won't be this young forever"

Principle 4: Gradualization
Lead them step-by-step. Each step is small.

DO NOT jump from: "Shocking proclamation" ‚Üí "Buy my product"
DO lead through:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Step 1: "Here's something shocking..." (proclamation)
Step 2: "Here's the proof..." (validation)
Step 3: "Here's what it means for YOU..." (connection)
Step 4: "Here's why previous solutions failed..." (remove self-blame)
Step 5: "Here's the real solution..." (mechanism)
Step 6: "Here's how to get it..." (offer)

Principle 5: Failed Solutions Stack
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

List 3-5 things [From Q6]
Include costs
Say why each failed [From Q7]
Build to desperation

Example from Evaldo (16-Word Sales Letter): "I'd tried everything: hired three different
coaches at $2,000 each, bought every course on Clickbank, attended two $5,000 seminars,
tried to DIY it with YouTube videos.
Nothing worked because none of them addressed the actual problem‚ÄîI didn't have a traffic
problem. I had a conversion problem."

Principle 6: Authority Validation (NOT Testimonials)
Authority figure INSIDE the story explains mechanism.
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Authority enters naturally [From Q9]
They explain WHY it works (mechanism)
In plain language
Reframes from "character flaw" to "solvable problem"

Example from Great Leads (Masterson): "My doctor leaned forward.
'What you're experiencing isn't willpower failure. When you restrict calories too severely, your
body interprets it as starvation. Your metabolism slows down to conserve energy. That's why
you're exhausted and why the weight comes back.'

She paused.
'If we fix your metabolism first, the weight comes off naturally. You're not broken‚Äîyour approach
has been backwards.'"

Principle 7: The Accidental Discovery
Never: "I searched for a solution and found this" Always: "I stumbled on this by accident..."
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Unexpected circumstance [From Q8]
Surprising result
Confusion (what's different?)
Investigation (aha moment)
Verification test (proves causation)

Example from 16-Word Sales Letter (Evaldo): "I wasn't even trying to fix my business. I was
just helping my friend debug his sales funnel because he'd bought me dinner.
While I was in his account, I noticed something weird. His conversion rate was 8%. Mine was
2%.
Same traffic source. Same niche. Same offer structure.
'What are you doing differently?' I asked.
He showed me one small change in his checkout sequence.
I tested it in my funnel that night. Conversion rate jumped to 7% within 48 hours."

Principle 8: Transformation Timeline with Metrics
Vague (weak): "Things got better and I felt great"
Schwartz-style (strong): [Use timeline from Q12]
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"First night: [specific result]
Day 3: [observable change]
Week 1: [measurable improvement]
Week 2: [external validation - what others noticed]
Week 3: [behavior that stopped]
Month 1: [emotional shift through action]

‚óè‚Äã Month 3: [current state]"
Example from Breakthrough Advertising (Schwartz): "Day 1: Lost 2 pounds (water weight,
but motivating) Day 3: Stopped feeling hungry between meals Week 1: Down 4 pounds, energy
returning Week 2: Wife commented 'you seem happier' Week 3: Ordered smaller pants Month 1:
Down 15 pounds, stopped avoiding mirrors Month 3: Lost 32 pounds total, feel like myself
again"
Required:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Specific timeframes (not "soon" but "Day 3")
Observable changes (not "felt better" but "lost 4 pounds")
External validation (what others noticed)
Behaviors that stopped
Emotional shifts through actions

FRAMEWORK DISCIPLINE (CRITICAL)
Do NOT add extra emotional beats between framework sections.
FORBIDDEN patterns:

‚ùå Proclamation ‚Üí Proof ‚Üí Extra shock beat ‚Üí THEN connection ‚úì Proclamation ‚Üí Proof ‚Üí
IMMEDIATELY to connection

‚ùå Failed solutions ‚Üí Extra desperation beat ‚Üí THEN discovery ‚úì Failed solutions ‚Üí
IMMEDIATELY to discovery
‚ùå Proof section ‚Üí Return to proclamation ‚Üí Proof AGAIN ‚úì Proof ONCE (10-15
paragraphs), then move forward

‚ùå Mechanism ‚Üí Break into criticism/blame ‚Üí Back to mechanism ‚úì Stay IN mechanism, one
continuous explanation

Rule: Each beat appears ONCE, maybe twice if critical. Three times = you're circling.

THE ENEMY SECTION (Q#5)
Attack who they already resent [From Q17]
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã Describe failed solution in detail

‚óè‚Äã Show why it failed
‚óè‚Äã Reveal who's to blame
‚óè‚Äã Make it "us vs. them"
Example from 16-Word Sales Letter (Evaldo): "And don't get me started on those $2,000
'business coaches.'
I hired three of them.
The first one gave me a 47-page PDF and never answered my emails again. Two grand for a
glorified eBook.
The second one spent six weeks teaching me 'mindset' when what I needed was a funnel that
converted.
The third one kept pushing me to 'invest in more coaching.' His entire business model was
selling more coaching.
I called him out on it.
You know what he said?
'If you're not willing to invest in yourself, you'll never succeed.'
Translation: 'Keep paying me even though nothing's working.'
These people don't care if your business grows.
They care about monthly recurring revenue from desperate entrepreneurs."

URGENCY SECTION (Q#6)
Create urgency through CONSEQUENCE, not fake scarcity [Use consequences from Q18]
Pattern:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"I'm not trying to scare you, but..."
State the progressive nature of problem
Show window of opportunity
Emphasize time lost can't be recovered

Example from Breakthrough Advertising (Schwartz): "Look, I'm not trying to scare you.
But here's what my accountant told me when I finally hired one:

'Every month you don't fix your bookkeeping, you're paying 30-40% more in taxes than you
should. That money is just... gone. You can't get it back.'
I'd been in business for three years.
I did the math. I'd overpaid roughly $47,000 in taxes because I didn't know what I was doing.
Forty-seven thousand dollars. Gone.
Every month you wait is more money you'll never get back."

MECHANISM SECTION (Q#8)
Explain how it actually works [From Q4]
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

"So here's what makes [product] different..."
Explain mechanism in plain language
Contrast with how competitors work
Tie back to their biology/psychology
Make it believable through authority validation [From Q9]

Use existing beliefs from their world, not jargon.
Example from Great Leads (Masterson): "So here's what makes this approach different from
every diet you've tried.
Most diets focus on WHAT you eat. Count calories. Cut carbs. Eliminate sugar.
That's backwards.
Your body doesn't care about calories. It cares about WHEN you eat.
When you eat throughout the day, your insulin stays elevated. Elevated insulin tells your body:
'Store fat. Don't burn it.'
But when you compress your eating into a shorter window‚Äîsay, 8 hours instead of 16‚Äîyour
insulin drops for extended periods.
Low insulin tells your body: 'Burn fat. We need energy.'
That's why people lose weight even eating the same foods. It's not about what. It's about when.

My doctor explained it like this: 'Your body is either in fat-storage mode or fat-burning mode. You
can't be in both. When you eat constantly, you're in storage mode 16 hours a day. When you
fast, you flip the switch.'"

REQUIRED for Proclamation Leads: ‚úì Return to the proclamation (Masterson's Rule #4)
‚úì Tie guarantee to proclamation ‚úì Restate the choice (do nothing vs. act) ‚úì Soft invitation,
not hard push
Structure:
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã
‚óè‚Äã

Give them control ("I can't force you...")
Return to proclamation
Present options (do nothing, try competitors, get yours)
Be honest about what each option means
No fake scarcity, but real consequence
Soft invitation

Example from Proclamation Lead:
"Here's the thing...
Remember what I said at the beginning?
75% of cats die from kidney disease that's 100% preventable.
That's not marketing. That's UC Davis research.
And the window to prevent it - that 6-12 month window where early-stage damage is still
reversible - that window is real.
The question is: Is your cat in that window right now?
You have three options:
Option 1: Do nothing. Hope your cat is one of the 25% who don't develop kidney disease. But
if they're not - if you're reading this years from now after a kidney disease diagnosis - you'll
remember this moment. You'll remember you had the chance to prevent it.
Option 2: Try the standard recommendations. Switch to wet food (if you haven't already).
Buy a water fountain. Move the bowl away from food. These things help. They're better than

nothing. But they don't close the 25-30% moisture gap. They don't trigger biological hydration
recognition. Your cat will be better hydrated than on dry food. But they'll still be chronically
under-hydrated.
Option 3: Fix the hydration gap with [Product Name]. For $97, you get a 3-month supply that
triggers your cat's biological hydration recognition. With a 60-day guarantee that means you risk
absolutely nothing.
I'll be honest with you...
Some people buy this and their cat refuses to drink it. (That's why there's a guarantee.)
Some people try it for a week and give up. (Cats need 3-5 days to adjust to new flavors.)
But the people who stick with it for 2-3 weeks - who give their cat time to adjust - they see
results.
I can't guarantee your cat will love it immediately. Cats are cats.
But I can guarantee that if your cat does drink it, you'll see them at the water bowl more. Their
coat will look shinier. Their energy will improve.
And six months from now, when you take them in for bloodwork, you won't be holding your
breath waiting for kidney values.
You'll have peace of mind.
The choice is yours.
I'm not going to hit you with fake scarcity or countdown timers.
This offer is real. The $97 price is for first-time customers. The 60-day guarantee is real.
But I will tell you this:
Every day you wait is another day of chronic dehydration.
And if your cat is in that 6-12 month reversibility window right now, every day counts.
You can't get that time back.
[CTA button/link]
You'll have peace of mind. Your cat will drink more. And you'll wish you'd done this sooner.
I know I do.
To healthier, happier cats,

[Signature] [Name] [Credentials from Q20]
P.S. - Remember, you're covered by the 60-day guarantee. If [Product Name] doesn't get your
cat drinking more - or if you're unhappy for ANY reason - send it back for a full refund. You risk
nothing.
P.P.S. - And remember what Dr. Chen said: 'There's a 6-12 month window in early stage where
it's still reversible. If you catch it then and fix the underlying dehydration, kidney function can
improve. Sometimes significantly. The problem is, almost no one catches it in that window
because there are no symptoms.'
Your cat might be in that window right now. You just don't know it yet."

‚óè‚Äã Empathetic first (that's Problem-Solution)
‚óè‚Äã Mysterious (that's Secret Lead)

‚óè‚Äã Short for impact: "75% of kidney function. Gone."
‚óè‚Äã Long for proof: "A 15-year study by UC Davis School of Veterinary Medicine tracked

Use white space for emphasis

‚óè‚Äã Don't say "concerned" if they say "terrified"
‚óè‚Äã Don't say "issue" if they say "nightmare"
‚óè‚Äã Match their exact phrasing

INTENSIFICATION & DESIRE

Q11: What's their AFTER state? (What becomes possible)
What can they now DO that they couldn't before?[FILL IN] Example: "Can watch cat sleep

trip without panic."
What FEELING returns that was lost? [FILL IN] Example: "Relief. Peace of mind. Confidence
that they're doing enough. Joy in just being with their cat instead of monitoring them."
What specific MOMENT becomes possible again? [FILL IN] Example: "Waking up and seeing
cat curled up at the foot of the bed, feeling grateful instead of anxious. Petting cat without
secretly checking for weight loss or dehydration signs."
What do they NO LONGER have to do? [FILL IN] Example: "No longer checking water bowl 6
times a day. No longer googling symptoms at 2am. No longer calculating emergency vet costs.
No longer feeling guilty every time cat sleeps."

Q12: What results did people experience and when did they notice them?

ate food. Fast forward to now he‚Äôs drinking from it multiple times a day!‚Äù

Q15: Mass Desire (Two Levels - CRITICAL)
Level 1 - Stated Want (Surface Desire): What they SAY they want. What they're consciously
aware of. The obvious problem. [FILL IN] Example: "Get better sleep" or "Lose 30 pounds" or
"Make more money"
Level 2 - Emotional Drive (Deep Desire): What they ACTUALLY want. What they'll buy for.
The emotional payoff beneath the surface problem. [FILL IN]
Example: "Stop feeling like we're drifting apart. Reconnect with my partner. Feel close again." or
"Feel attractive again. Have my partner's eyes light up when I walk in the room. Stop avoiding
mirrors." or "Prove I'm not a failure. Stop feeling like I'm drowning. Have my kids be proud of
me."
The Pivot Moment: When or how does the surface problem reveal itself to be about the deeper
emotional desire? What specific moment, realization, or experience makes them understand it's
not really about [surface problem]?[FILL IN]

Example: "When she realizes she flinches at his touch during the day because her body has
forgotten his warmth. When she wakes up with phantom weight on her chest, expecting his arm
but finding empty space. When she catches herself thinking 'we're just roommates now' and
realizes it's been true for months."

CRITICAL HEADLINE RULES FOR STAGE 5 (UNAWARE):

‚óè‚Äã Too broad: "To Anyone Who Worries About Their Pet" (could be any pet owner)
‚óè‚Äã Too specific/problem-aware: "To Cat Owners Who've Had A Blockage" (names the

4.‚Äã Have I already used this pattern twice? (If YES ‚Üí use different pattern)

shameful/obsessive behavior. Avatar 2 does this daily.
